title: Aureline extension publisher-lifecycle-event and installed-package lifecycle-notification vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for publisher-lifecycle events (verification, transfer, suspension, abandonment, revocation, quarantine, restoration, mirror promotion) and the installed-package lifecycle notifications they fan out to. Reuses the registry / mirror / offline-bundle / publisher-continuity vocabulary frozen by schemas/extensions/registry_manifest.schema.json and schemas/extensions/effective_permission.schema.json, the discovery-row vocabulary frozen by schemas/extensions/discovery_ranking_reason.schema.json, the badge / warning vocabulary frozen by schemas/extensions/discovery_badge.schema.json, and the anti-abuse register at artifacts/extensions/anti_abuse_states.yaml. Discovery, install-review, installed-row, support-export, and moderation surfaces project these records verbatim; minting per-surface lifecycle vocabulary is non-conforming. Adding a new lifecycle_event_class, lifecycle_actor_class, lifecycle_reason_class, lifecycle_effect_*_class, installed_package_notification_disposition_class, or lifecycle_audit_event_id is additive-minor with a publisher_lifecycle_event_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw artifact bytes, raw signing-key material, raw attestation-bundle bytes, raw URLs, raw publisher-private data, raw repository paths, and raw popularity counts MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs and class labels only.

publisher_lifecycle_event_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this publisher-lifecycle vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

extension_identity.description: Namespaced extension id of the form 'publisher_id/extension_id' (mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json).

semver_string.description: SemVer string the lifecycle row resolves to. Pinning is a policy-pack concern; this field carries the declared candidate version.

registry_source_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/registry_source_class verbatim. A lifecycle event MUST name the registry source it applies to; private-registry events do not bypass disclosure simply because they are internal.

redaction_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/redaction_class. Raw artifact bytes, raw URLs, raw repository paths, raw publisher-private fields MUST NOT appear on any sink.

lifecycle_event_class.description: Closed lifecycle-event-class vocabulary. Every lifecycle event carries exactly one class. The set covers verification, transfer, suspension, abandonment, revocation (emergency and administrative), quarantine, restoration, artifact-level emergency disable / revocation, and mirror promotion. Discovery, install-review, installed-row, and support-export surfaces project these classes verbatim; minting per-surface lifecycle classes is non-conforming.

lifecycle_actor_class.description: Closed actor-class vocabulary. Every event carries exactly one actor class so support-export and moderation review surfaces can render a typed actor chip. Raw user identity is never carried; the per-actor identity ref rides actor_identity_ref.

lifecycle_reason_class.description: Closed reason-class vocabulary. Every event names a typed reason. A row without a typed reason is non-conforming; honesty demands reason_class_unknown_requires_review over an empty value.

lifecycle_effect_on_trust_badge_class.description: Closed effect-on-trust-badge vocabulary. The lifecycle event's effect on the publisher / artifact trust badge that discovery, installed rows, and install-review render. Surfaces MUST project this class verbatim; silent retention of a high trust badge after a revocation / quarantine / suspension event is denied with installed_package_stale_high_trust_badge.

lifecycle_effect_on_compatibility_badge_.description: Closed effect-on-compatibility-badge vocabulary. After a publisher transfer / restoration / suspension the compatibility claim MAY require reverification before the row credits compatibility-first; this class names the projected effect.

lifecycle_effect_on_runtime_cost_badge_c.description: Closed effect-on-runtime-cost-badge vocabulary. Quarantine engagement forces the quarantined badge; quarantine clearance restores the prior class only after a fresh activation-evidence packet.

lifecycle_effect_on_anti_abuse_state_cla.description: Closed effect-on-anti-abuse-state vocabulary. The lifecycle event's effect on the anti-abuse register row(s) bound to the publisher / artifact. A surface that hides a widened or engaged anti-abuse state is denied with review_disclosure_incomplete.

installed_package_notification_dispositi.description: Closed installed-package notification-disposition vocabulary. Every notification fanned out to an installed row carries exactly one disposition. A revocation / quarantine / emergency-disable lifecycle event MUST NOT resolve to notify_only_no_action_required; doing so denies with installed_package_stale_high_trust_badge.

installed_package_repair_affordance_clas.description: Closed installed-package repair-affordance vocabulary. Every notification carries at least one typed repair affordance so the user / admin always has a typed next step; silent dead-ends are non-conforming.

lifecycle_audit_event_id.description: Frozen lifecycle audit-event-id vocabulary. Additive-minor. Raw artifact bytes, raw signing-key material, raw attestation bytes, and raw publisher-private data MUST NOT appear on any event.

lifecycle_denial_reason_class.description: Closed lifecycle denial-reason vocabulary. Silent fallback to a generic 'publisher event' chip is forbidden; every denial emits the corresponding audit event with a typed reason and a repair-affordance label.

publisher_continuity_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0012 publisher_continuity_row the event applies to. Required even on artifact-scoped revocation / emergency disable events so installed-row notifications resolve back to the same publisher row.

successor_publisher_continuity_ref.description: Ref to the successor publisher's continuity row when lifecycle_event_class is publisher_transfer_announced / publisher_transfer_completed. Null otherwise.

subject_registry_endpoint_ref.description: Ref to the registry endpoint record (public registry, approved mirror, private registry, offline bundle id). Null when subject_registry_source_class is local_archive or quarantined_local_copy.

subject_extension_identity.description: Namespaced extension id when the event is artifact-scoped (artifact_revocation_engaged, artifact_emergency_disable_engaged, artifact_emergency_disable_cleared). Null when the event is publisher-scoped.

subject_extension_version.description: Subject artifact version when artifact-scoped. Null otherwise.

subject_registry_manifest_row_ref.description: Ref to the registry_manifest_row when the event is artifact-scoped. Null when publisher-scoped.

mirror_continuity_row_ref.description: Ref to the mirror_continuity_row when lifecycle_event_class is mirror_promotion_admitted or mirror_promotion_revoked. Null otherwise.

actor_identity_ref.description: Ref to the actor identity row (operator decision row, admin decision row, automated detector row). Raw user identity / email never carried.

reason_label.description: Human-legible reason rendered on review surfaces. Raw publisher-private material forbidden.

effective_at.description: Effective date of the lifecycle event. Discovery, installed-row, install-review, and support-export surfaces render this date verbatim; back-dating is forbidden by the surface (the schema pins the field's presence).

prior_publisher_lifecycle_event_record_r.description: Ref to the prior lifecycle event the current event amends or supersedes. Required (non-null) for publisher_restoration_after_review, publisher_suspension_lifted, publisher_quarantine_cleared, publisher_abandonment_cleared, artifact_emergency_disable_cleared, mirror_promotion_revoked, and publisher_verification_revalidated; null otherwise.

anti_abuse_state_row_refs.description: Refs to anti_abuse_state_id rows from artifacts/extensions/anti_abuse_states.yaml engaged, widened, narrowed, or cleared by this event. A surface that hides an engaged or widened state is denied with review_disclosure_incomplete.

policy_pack_applicable_refs.description: Refs to policy_pack_constraint_row rows whose scope covers the publisher / artifact at the moment of the event.

evidence_refs.description: Refs to the rows that justify the event (signing-continuity row, attestation-bundle ref, moderation review row, anti-abuse signal entry source, mirror continuity row, policy-pack constraint row). Raw evidence bodies never carried.

installed_package_notification_required.description: True when the event MUST fan out an installed_package_lifecycle_notification_record to every installed copy. False only for events that have no installed-row impact (publisher_verification_admitted on a brand-new publisher with no installed copies; publisher_inactivity_cleared on a publisher whose installed copies were already up to date).

denial_reason_class.description: Denial reason rendered on install / installed-row review when the event blocks an install path (publisher_quarantine_engaged, publisher_revocation_*, artifact_revocation_engaged, artifact_emergency_disable_engaged, publisher_suspension_pending_review). no_denial_admissible on advisory / informational events.

allOf.description: Publisher-scoped events MUST have null subject_extension_identity, null subject_extension_version, and null subject_registry_manifest_row_ref. Artifact-scoped events MUST have non-null values for all three.
Mirror-promotion events MUST cite a non-null mirror_continuity_row_ref.
Mirror-promotion events MUST have subject_registry_source_class = approved_mirror.
Publisher-transfer events MUST cite a non-null successor_publisher_continuity_ref.
Publisher-verification-admitted MUST promote the trust badge to verified, managed_approved, or organisational; it MUST NOT leave the badge unchanged or demote it.

Publisher-revocation, publisher-suspension-pending-review, artifact-revocation, and artifact-emergency-disable-engaged events MUST demote the trust badge to unverified or force quarantined-cannot-inherit; leaving the badge unchanged is non-conforming.
Publisher-quarantine-engaged MUST force trust_badge_forced_quarantined_cannot_inherit and engage the anti-abuse register row.
Publisher-transfer-completed MUST cap the rendered trust badge at organisational until the successor publisher row clears its first verification review.
Publisher-restoration-after-review MUST cite a non-null prior_publisher_lifecycle_event_record_ref and MUST NOT leave the trust badge unchanged.

Publisher-quarantine-cleared, publisher-suspension-lifted, publisher-abandonment-cleared, artifact-emergency-disable-cleared, mirror-promotion-revoked, and publisher-verification-revalidated MUST cite a non-null prior_publisher_lifecycle_event_record_ref so the cleared event resolves back to the engaging event.

Publisher-revocation, publisher-quarantine-engaged, artifact-revocation, artifact-emergency-disable-engaged, and publisher-suspension-pending-review events MUST cite a typed denial_reason_class other than no_denial_admissible.

Publisher-revocation, publisher-quarantine-engaged, publisher-suspension-pending-review, artifact-revocation, artifact-emergency-disable-engaged, and publisher-transfer-completed events MUST set installed_package_notification_required = true so installed copies cannot retain stale high-trust badges.
Quarantine-clearance and emergency-disable-clearance events MUST narrow or clear the anti-abuse state, never widen or engage it.
Mirror-promotion-admitted MUST set effect_on_trust_badge_class to either trust_badge_unchanged or trust_badge_demoted_to_unverified; a mirror promotion never widens trust above the origin tier.

publisher_lifecycle_event_record.description: One publisher-lifecycle event. Discovery, install-review, installed-row, support-export, and moderation surfaces project the same record; minting per-surface lifecycle vocabulary is non-conforming.

publisher_lifecycle_event_record_ref.description: Ref to the publisher_lifecycle_event_record this notification fans out from. Raw event bodies never carried; the notification reads the typed effect_*_class fields from the event.

installed_workspace_ref.description: Ref to the workspace where the installed copy lives. Raw workspace path / id never carried.

installed_registry_source_class.description: Registry source class of the installed copy. Discovery and installed-row surfaces render this verbatim; private-registry installs MUST NOT bypass lifecycle disclosure on the basis of being internal.

installed_registry_manifest_row_ref.description: Ref to the registry_manifest_row the installed copy resolved against at install time. Null when the installed copy is a local_archive_import_row.

installed_local_archive_ref.description: Ref to the local_archive_import_row when the installed copy was imported as a local archive or quarantined local copy. Null otherwise.

repair_affordance_class_set.description: At least one typed repair affordance the user / admin can take from the installed-row notification. A notification with no repair affordance is non-conforming.

rendered_label.description: Human-legible label rendered on the installed-row notification card. Raw publisher-private material forbidden.

explanation_label.description: Inspectable explanation rendered when the user opens the notification. Raw publisher-private material forbidden.

trust_badge_after_event_class.description: Projected trust-badge state on the installed row after the event. Discovery and installed-row surfaces MUST render this class verbatim; silent retention of a high-trust badge after a revocation / quarantine / suspension event is denied with installed_package_stale_high_trust_badge.

denial_reason_class.description: Denial reason rendered on the installed-row review when the notification disposition blocks an install / activation path. no_denial_admissible on advisory / informational notifications.

acknowledged_at.description: Timestamp the user / admin acknowledged the notification, when applicable. Null when the disposition is notify_only_no_action_required or when the notification has not yet been acknowledged.

allOf.description: notify_with_uninstall_required_no_recovery MUST cite repair_affordance_class_set containing both no_recovery_review_only and uninstall_now_no_recovery, and denial_reason_class != no_denial_admissible.
notify_with_install_disabled_pending_review MUST cite a denial_reason_class other than no_denial_admissible.
notify_with_required_review_pending MUST cite a denial_reason_class other than no_denial_admissible (review is pending; the install path is gated).

notify_only_no_action_required MUST be paired with denial_reason_class = no_denial_admissible. A revocation / quarantine / emergency-disable / suspension event that resolves the installed row to notify_only_no_action_required is denied with installed_package_stale_high_trust_badge.

When trust_badge_after_event_class is trust_badge_demoted_to_unverified or trust_badge_forced_quarantined_cannot_inherit, the disposition MUST NOT be notify_only_no_action_required or notify_with_optional_update_offered.
Installed copies sourced from local_archive or quarantined_local_copy MUST cite a non-null installed_local_archive_ref.
Installed copies sourced from public_registry, approved_mirror, private_registry, or offline_bundle MUST cite a non-null installed_registry_manifest_row_ref.

installed_package_lifecycle_notification.description: One notification fanned out to one installed copy after a publisher-lifecycle event. Discovery, installed-row, install-review, and support-export surfaces project the same record; private-registry installs do not bypass lifecycle disclosure simply because they are internal.
